Shiyu Yang

More Than
Just Games:Learning from
Video Games

Video games are now not just about playing around. Nintendo Switch has helped me have a chance to play different games which, in turn, allowed me to acquire different and useful skills that I can use in a real-life environment.

Photograph by Shiyu Yang

What I Learned

Key Takeaways

01

Teamwork

Overcooked taught communication, role-sharing, and staying calm under pressure.

Let's Go
02

Problem-Solving

Language Learning

Let's Go
· · ✿ · ·
01
Teamwork & Communication

Overcooked

A chaotic kitchen. A single lesson in collaboration.

Real-time communication, flexible role-sharing and patience are often more important than personal skills in Co-op games like Overcooked.

Overcooked — Team17, 2016
Photograph by Shiyu Yang

Key Points

  • Communicate clearly with teammates
  • Adjust roles when situations change
  • Stay patient under pressure

My Reflection

In Overcooked, I play with other people to carry out different chores in the kitchen. Teamwork is an area in which I learned a lot in this game. You cannot afford to win when you are just taking care of your job, like chopping the vegetables or putting the food on the plate. The kitchen is ever-changing. The floor is moving in certain situations, or there is a separation of pathways, or cases where items are moved to other areas. The entire team becomes a mess, and we are unable to complete orders without communicating with one another and collaborating with them. That is why we have to stay in touch. We remind one another of our missing ingredients, who does what, or change our jobs as and when it is necessary. In other cases, we are also called upon to be flexible when the kitchen layout alters. We are certain that all people are in the correct place to work. We eliminate the repetition of tasks by dividing the work in a proper manner and work more productively. This resembles real-life projects, which are carried out in groups. I have learned how to remain calm, be a talker, and observer of what my teammates are up to so that we can achieve our objectives easily. The experience with Overcooked served to learn that teamwork process both during the games and in real life has to be supported by communication, cooperation, and patience.
02
Problem-Solving & Creative Thinking

The Legend
of Zelda

No right path. Infinite strategies.

Zelda gave me permission to be wrong. There are different ways to hit enemies. If one attempt fails, it doesn't mean that I can't do it. I just need to try again in another way. It taught me to stop and observe when I encounter difficulties, and then rethink the next step.

The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild — Nintendo, 2017
Photograph by Shiyu Yang

Key Points

  • Try different solutions creatively
  • Observe before rushing into action
  • Adapt strategies when needed

My Reflection

Problem-solving is the most positive skill that I acquired during The Legend of Zelda. The game has adequate puzzles, dungeons, and enemies. Each of the tasks has several solutions to be made. Initially, I would experiment with various techniques and find out what works. I was amazed sometimes to discover a new method for solving something. This experience of trial and error showed me that you cannot simply act when dealing with a difficult issue. The other thing you should do is to look around and think of the situation first. There were times that I would become trapped in a shrine or a dungeon, and it could not be completed simply through perseverance. During such moments, my thoughts would be on various solutions or refer to guides to have an insight into other players. This served as a lesson to me that it is not only about getting things over and done when it comes to solving problems. The Legend of Zelda provides the player with much freedom. The game has no right way to complete it. This implies that every player is able to solve problems his or her way. You are required to think on your own, be it where to go, what to use, or what puzzles to solve. It is similar to a research project or group work in university. Problem-solving abilities improved significantly through free exploration and creative thinking that were available in the game. I was taught to think, attempt, and reformulate my tactics when things are unpredictable.
03
Language Learning & Reading Skills

Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney Trilogy

When learning a new language feels boring, try learning it differently.

Ace Attorney made me realize that trying different learning methods can make learning more interesting. In the process of playing games, my vocabulary and reading comprehension have been improved, and I don't feel like I'm learning at all.

Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney Trilogy — Capcom, 2014
Photograph by Shiyu Yang

Key Points

  • Practice English in an enjoyable way
  • Learn vocabulary through context
  • Improve reading focus and detail

My Reflection

There are plenty of games in multiple languages available on the Switch. Games give me a chance to train in English. In my case, it is uncomfortable when learning a language becomes boring. Previously, I had problems about reading. I would read scholarly sources, but I forget I need to understand it. During a certain period, I did not want to read long texts to study English. Instead, I chose to learn English through playing games. The English game I decided to practice was Ace Attorney. The game is nearly completely dialogue-driven, and the words are not that difficult. I can easily look up the words that are very hard to pronounce. The fact that some sections are not understood does not make me stop playing the game. The change is that in the process of playing while learning new words. This way, I will be able to learn the language in a comfortable setting and build my vocabulary. This habit taught me to be more attentive when reading English works. With the help of Ace Attorney, I realized that my language abilities will get better on their own, and I will be interested. I also got to know how to pay attention to the key points in reading and understanding.
Final Reflection

What this project taught me about learning itself

These games that I have learnt significant skills in one way or another. Overcooked helped me to understand the importance of teamwork and communication. The Legend of Zelda helped me to develop the skills of problem solving and creative thinking. Ace Attorney enabled me to have a nice experience of learning English and sharpening my concentration. Most of these skills can be used not only on the field, but also in life, at school. I got to know that both entertainment and learning can be combined using gaming. Proficiencies gained in this case through games will be applied to my future practice and studies. It will not be a waste of time when you extract something important out of the video games.

Skills Demonstrated

Teamwork Communication Problem-Solving Critical Thinking Language Skills Creativity Reflection Research Adaptability
